title: Stage 10 — Image-ad concepts
description: Typed I/O contract for /mkt-creative-run Stage 10 (design §2/§6.1 — no free-text handoffs). Stage 10 expands the storyboard / brief into a set of static image-ad concepts for one persona (typically ~6). Each concept names its angle, headline, sub-copy, the visual description (the image-prompt seed the generator expands), size, CTA, and every claim it asserts (claim_refs). As in Stage 09, claim/number text is a deterministic caption overlay, never model-rendered — claim_refs travel with the concept. provenance: run_id + persona_ref.

claimRef.description: A reference to one approved/declared claim id in context/claims.json (e.g. "CLM-008"). The bare id only — render markers ([[...]]/{{...}}) are presentation. The deterministic claims floor reads these to fail-close on unverified claims.

claimRefs.description: Every claim id this concept's copy (headline + sub_copy + cta) asserts (empty when the concept makes no PrepEdu claim). Typed surface for the claims gate — never inferred from prose.

run_id.description: Timestamped run id (e.g. 20260608-072919) — ties this artifact to the run manifest.

persona_ref.description: Persona these concepts target, referencing context/proposed/audience-personas.md (e.g. "Persona 4 — Thảo (Segment A)").

concepts.description: The image-ad concepts (typically ~6 distinct angles on the same persona). Each is independently shippable as a static image ad.

concept_id.description: Stable id of the concept within this run (e.g. "IMG-1"). Lets the judge rank concepts.

angle.description: The strategic angle / motivator this concept leads on (the why-this-concept-exists trace).

headline.description: Primary on-image headline (locale-native). Copy-bearing → its claims are in claim_refs.

sub_copy.description: Supporting sub-copy (locale-native). Empty string when the concept uses headline only. Copy-bearing → claims in claim_refs.

visual_desc.description: The visual description / image-prompt seed the generator expands. Claim/number text is NEVER baked here — it is overlaid as a deterministic caption (see claim_refs).

size.description: Output size / placement spec (e.g. "1080x1080" square in-feed, "1080x1920" 9:16 story).

cta.description: The call-to-action text (locale-native). Copy-bearing; any claim it carries is listed in claim_refs.
